I participated in a recent content campaign, writing a deep dive about web3 community building. I do not have a massive audience. My account is relatively small. On any legacy app, that post would have been buried by the algorithm within ten minutes.

But here is what actually happened on Rally. The AI scoring evaluated the substance of my writing, not my stats. I woke up to on-chain rewards dropping into my wallet. No negotiating with PR reps. No waiting 60 days for a wire transfer. I did the work, the protocol recognized the quality, and I got paid.

It completely changes the dynamic of why we create. You stop performing for an algorithm and start writing for real impact.

How many hours have you wasted pitching brands that ghosted you just because your follower count was not big enough?

text:
You've Been Writing on the Internet for Years, But Do You Actually Own Anything You Wrote?

Think about it for a second.

Every article you've ever published on Medium.

Every long thread you've spent hours writing on X.

Every guide you've shared.

Every tutorial that took you days to research.

Who actually owns it?

Most people will instinctively say,

"Me."

But that's not entirely true.

Because the moment a centralized platform changes its rules, suspends your account, disappears, or simply decides your content no longer belongs on its platform.

Years of your work can vanish overnight.

That's the internet we've accepted for decades.

We create.

Platforms own.

Recently I came across a project on @SuiNetwork called @inkray_io , and it made me realize something.

Maybe we've been publishing the wrong way all along.

Instead of treating articles like temporary posts sitting inside someone else's database, Inkray treats every piece of writing as something you actually own.

Not metaphorically.

Literally.

When you publish an article, it isn't just uploaded to another company's server.

It's stored permanently using @WalrusProtocol , linked to your identity on Sui, and placed inside your own personal Vault.

That means your article belongs to you.

Not Inkray.

Not a company.

Not an algorithm.

You.

And that's a much bigger shift than people realize.

Think about today's creator economy.

Most writers spend years building an audience.

But they're constantly living inside rented houses.

Medium can change its policies.

Substack can change its business model.

A platform can remove content.

Algorithms can decide whether anyone even sees what you spent hours writing.

You're creating value...

but someone else controls the distribution.

Inkray flips that idea.

Your wallet becomes your account.

Your Vault becomes your library.

Your articles become permanent digital assets instead of temporary database entries.

Even better.

They're building far beyond simple publishing.

Writers can create paid subscriptions.

Receive reader tips directly.

Publish premium encrypted articles.

Turn their best work into collectible digital assets.

Even import their existing Medium articles with a single RSS sync instead of starting from zero.

That means your previous work doesn't have to stay trapped somewhere else.

Readers benefit too.

No ads chasing clicks.

No engagement farming.

No algorithm deciding what deserves attention.

Instead, readers discover articles through communities, follows, and curated feeds.

Good writing wins because it's good.

Not because someone mastered the algorithm.

The infrastructure behind it is equally interesting.

Sui provides the speed and on-chain ownership.

Walrus provides decentralized storage, meaning articles remain available across a distributed network instead of relying on a single server.

SEAL handles encryption, allowing premium content to stay private until a subscriber unlocks it.

Each piece solves a different problem.

Together they create something that feels less like another blogging platform...

and more like permanent digital publishing.

The platform is currently live on Sui Testnet, and the team is rolling out early access through invite codes.

I managed to secure one of those invites.

If you're a writer, researcher, educator, or someone who genuinely enjoys creating long-form content, this is probably worth trying before everyone else discovers it.

Because I genuinely think we're approaching a point where creators won't ask,

Which platform should I post on?

Instead they'll ask,

Which platform actually lets me own what I create?

That feels like a much more important question.

Maybe that's exactly where publishing is heading.

And honestly.

I'm excited to watch it unfold.

text:
A blogger who was instrumental in bringing down a Walter Mitty chief constable has been discovered dead – a month after he was convicted of child sex offences.

Simon Tilley was reportedly found dead in his hotel room on Friday after failing to check out.

His blogs on Northamptonshire Police were instrumental in the exposure of former chief constable Nick Adderley - who falsely claimed he had served in the Falklands and had made other untrue claims on his CV.

Adderley, 56, was sacked from his high-profile post in 2024 after a disciplinary panel ruled that he had engaged in 'sustained, brazen dishonesty'.

He has since been charged with fraud and misconduct in public office following allegations that he made false claims about his military service and educational achievements when applying to work for the police.

During Tilley's trial in May, a court heard he had been caught sending explicit messages and pornographic videos to an undercover Northamptonshire Police detective, who the defendant thought was a 13-year-old boy called 'Harry'.

Tilley was found guilty of two child sex offences early last month.

He was also found guilty of stalking the wife of the detective involved in investigating the sex case, herself also a police officer.

He posted a picture of her wedding day and marriage certificate online along with false allegations she was corrupt.

Jurors heard he also called the lead detective a sex offender, branded him 'PC Pervert' online and wrote him a letter which 'quite unnecessarily' included a reference to knowing his home address and current partner.

Stafford Crown Court heard Tilley carried out the stalking campaign in a bid 'to derail the criminal proceedings', prosecutors said, and he had obtained the marriage certificates of at least ten officers.

Tilley was found guilty of two child sex offences, perverting the course of justice and one count of stalking. He was found not guilty of a second stalking charge. He was bailed ahead of sentencing on September 18.

Tilley, of Kingsthorpe Hollow, Northampton, is believed to have gone to the Hampton by Hilton hotel in nearby Corby last Thursday and posted about his trip to his Instagram account.

But on Friday when he failed to check out, hotel staff went to his room and discovered him dead.

Northamptonshire Police said: 'Police officers were called to the sudden death of a 46-year-old man in a hotel room in Corby, at about 12.35pm on Friday, July 3.

'We are not treating his death as suspicious and will be preparing a report for the coroner's office.'

The Northamptonshire Telegraph reported that up until last week, Tilley had been emailing its journalists with details of perceived failures in force procedures and cases involving other police officers.

Link to the article: https://t.co/UC1Ji5z1p9

text:
🚨🇬🇧 Algorithm power grab: UK pushes plan to force-feed BBC propaganda

The British government is pushing plans to force platforms like YouTube and TikTok to boost establishment media, including the BBC, ITV, and Channel 4, in algorithmic rankings.

Officials justify the move with claims about "misinformation" and an "existential threat to democracy".

Television "is key to supporting social cohesion and is a cornerstone of our democracy," Lisa Nandy, the Culture Secretary, claims.
🤔 The real agenda

The real reason behind the move is that the UK government is losing narrative control and is using legislation to shape what the public sees online.

▪️Government wants "trusted news providers" hand-picked and boosted by algorithms.

▪️Extra visibility would be enforced during crises or periods of unrest.

▪️The proposal comes amid public service broadcasters' statements about their content being drowned out by independent creators.

▪️The government's Green Paper is part of a wider push to help traditional media compete with streamers.

▪️The Online Safety Act currently only targets illegal content, not "misinformation", and doesn't force platforms to remove harmful but lawful claims.

😨 The fallout 

▪️The new law enables the state to effectively define what is "reliable" and what counts as "misinformation".

▪️The definition of misinformation remains vague, politically flexible.

▪️Social media platforms are likely to resist these rules, seen as algorithmic interference and censorship by proxy.

▪️With a majority of UK adults and roughly 75% of 16–24-year-olds getting news via social media (as per Ofcom), ranking control effectively means control over the flow of information.

💩 BBC fake news: Greatest hits

▪️During the 1984 miners' strike, the BBC edited footage out of order to make protesters look like the aggressors, claiming protesters attacked police first. It admitted the "mistake" only seven years later.

▪️In 2015, it ran an article on Gaza tunnels that described Israeli military analyst Eado Hecht as "independent" without disclosing his ties to the military establishment. The BBC later ruled this broke its accuracy rules.

▪️In 2024, over 100 BBC staff and more than 200 media professionals signed an open letter slamming the corporation for giving too much weight to Israeli official accounts, failing to scrutinise Israeli government claims, and leaving out historical context.

▪️In 2025, Panorama spliced clips of US President Donald Trump's January 6 speech, alleging he directly called for violence. The BBC apologised, pulled the episode, and both the Director General and Head of News had to resign. Trump is now suing the corp for billions.

ui/ux, research, architecture, branding, visual storytelling, image generation

production, coordination, operations, experimentation

agents that don't loop don't scale

one prompt → one answer → stops

that's not an agency. that's a very expensive to-do list

the missing piece is loops — agents that run, check their own output, self-correct, and hand back to the next agent without a human in the middle

that's what turns this repo from a demo into something that actually ships

text:
It seems impossible to avoid AI slop.

But I've found a way.

Stay off social media feeds.

Read books instead.

The older the better.

No one puts as much effort into a social media post as an author puts into a book.

Especially now with AI.

But that's not the only problem with social media content.

Feeds are the most competitive battlefields for attention that exist.

This creates perverse incentives.

Ones that lead creators to use the darkest arts of persuasion to their advantage (and your detriment).

Fear.

FOMO.

Outrage.

Whatever it takes for a like, comment, or share.

(Oh, yeah, don't forget to hit that follow button if you're enjoying this post.)

And don't even get me started on the platforms themselves.

They have endless data on what gets attention, and they use it to hijack your mind.

Their algorithms bias toward recency and speed.

It doesn't make sense for creators to invest too much time to create content.

Why bother when your stuff will only get seen for a few hours or days at most?

Books aren't like that.

Birthing a book is a masochistic process.

Think about the contrast: the author is usually attempting to share decades of their knowledge and experience, distilled through years of grueling writing and editing. Multiple minds shaping the words, just so it can be cleanly packaged to be consumed by you in a few hours.

That massive investment of time is exactly why books offer a completely different level of quality, both in the value and clarity of the writing.

I’ve spent the last few weeks re-reading advertising classics from the 1920s and 30s by Claude Hopkins and John Caples.

Astonishingly, most of the lessons they wrote about back then still apply to advertising today.

What would you rather? Consume information that's survived through the ages, or content curated by the algos?

The court imposed a 5-year sentence for "assembly and collusion to act against national security" and 13 years for "propaganda activity contrary to national security" under Article 4 of the draconian Law on Intensifying Punishments for Espionage.

Alikordi was arrested by security forces at his law office in Mashhad on December 12, 2025, hours after posting an Instagram Live in which he criticized the violent arrest of mourners attending the seventh-day memorial for his brother, Khosrow Alikordi, a human rights lawyer who died under suspicious circumstances earlier that month.

During the livestream, Alikordi said security forces had stormed the memorial service, beaten mourners, deployed tear gas, and arrested multiple attendees. He also challenged the state's account of his brother's death and said he possessed confidential documents related to his case.

Javad Alikordi has previously faced multiple politically motivated prosecutions, imprisonment, electronic monitoring, travel bans, and restrictions on both his legal practice and online activities.

🔸 Independent lawyers in Iran have faced an escalating crackdown, with authorities routinely sentencing lawyers to disbarment, internal exile, or imprisonment solely for defending victims of state violence and exposing judicial abuses.

🔸 The 18-year prison sentence imposed on Javad Alikordi marks a significant escalation in the Iranian authorities' campaign to criminalize the legal profession and intimidate lawyers who challenge state abuses. 

🔹 CHRI warns that Iran's judiciary has become a central instrument of repression, systematically targeting activists, independent lawyers, families seeking justice, journalists, and dissidents through politically motivated prosecutions and abusive national security charges.

The international community's failure to respond to systematic human rights violations in Iran has allowed authorities to intensify domestic repression with impunity.

text:
Adult Swim Expands the 'Rick and Morty' Universe with Earth-Bound Spin-Off, 'President Curtis':

The Oval Office is getting a sci-fi makeover. Following years of quiet development, Adult Swim is officially expanding its flagship animated franchise with President Curtis, a brand-new spin-off series centring on the Commander-in-Chief's extraterrestrial and paranormal exploits.

The new programme is set to premiere on 26 July 2026, directly following the Rick and Morty season nine finale.

From Interdimensional Travel to Domestic Monster Hunting

Co-created by Rick and Morty veterans Dan Harmon and James Siciliano, the series sees Keith David reprising his fan-favourite role as President Andre Curtis. However, fans expecting a carbon copy of the parent show’s intergalactic hijinks will find a distinct shift in tone.

While Rick Sanchez is off traversing the multiverse and mingling with alien hiveminds, President Curtis will be dealing with distinctly Earth-bound oddities. Harmon outlined the creative divergence between the two programmes:

"The comparison I make is it’s like Doctor Who and The X-Files. Both involve aliens, but you have a different perspective there. It allows the tonality to stay the same, but it’s not redundant."

Expect the President to be engaging in domestic defence against classic cryptids and folklore staples—including werewolves, vampires, Paul Bunyan, and the Loch Ness Monster.

A Reluctant Leader in a Parallel Reality

Interestingly, the spin-off retcons the President's personality to better suit a leading role. Rather than the approval-obsessed politician seen clashing with Rick, the series reframes him as a reluctant, dutiful soldier who views the presidency as a necessary burden to protect the populace. According to the creators, he refuses to lie or cheat for poll numbers, offering an escapist "parallel reality" that leans closer to an homage to The West Wing rather than biting political satire.

Will Rick and Morty Appear?

For fans wondering about crossovers, Harmon and Siciliano confirmed that the titular duo will make a brief, meta appearance in the pilot episode to acknowledge the spin-off. However, this will be a "one and done" cameo for the first season, deliberately giving President Curtis the breathing room it needs to establish its own unique staff, world, and identity on the network.

Article by @DEADLINE, link on the following post.

text:
Okay so... I quit my job. 😮‍💨
Not "thinking about it," not "planning to eventually" I actually did it. Handed in my notice, closed that chapter, and I'm not looking back. It's been a long time coming and honestly writing this still feels surreal.
For a while now I've been underpaid for the work I was putting in, working hours that ate up my entire day, in an environment that left me drained instead of motivated. I kept telling myself "just push through, it'll get better." It never did. I was giving everything to something that gave nothing back  so I decided if I'm going to work this hard, I'd rather do it for myself.
I put my own savings into building this properly  new accounts, new setup, everything I needed to run this as a real business, not just a side thing. No safety net. No backup plan. I burned that option on purpose so I'd have no choice but to make this work.
I'm also doing this from Asia, trying to build a name for myself in a space mostly dominated by creators from the US, Japan, and Korea. Different time zones, different platforms, different payment systems. It's an uphill climb, I know. But I'd rather struggle doing something I love than stay comfortable in something that was wearing me down.
This is MochiBrush 🍡 my art brand. VTuber emotes, PNG models, rigging, stream overlays. What used to be a late nights and weekends side thing is now the actual plan. This is the job now.
Made a lil Linktree so everything's in one place  my socials, my art, and a way to support MochiBrush directly if you're feeling generous. 🍡✨ Come say hi, check out my work, or drop a tip link in bio 💜
I won't pretend I have this figured out. Some days I'm excited, some days I'm scared out of my mind. But staying would've been the bigger risk.
If you've read this far  thank you, genuinely. New art, new emotes, and a lot more of me showing up here from now on. Let's see where this goes 🥹✨
#smallartistsupport #vgen #artistsontwitter #vtubers

𝐉𝐚𝐦𝐞𝐬 𝐂𝐡𝐢𝐧

====================

============================
Large-Scale Religious Conversions in Sabah under the Administrations of Tun Datu Mustapha Harun and Syed Kechik, (1970–1975)
By Avtar Singh
Before the twentieth century, Islam had already reached the coastal regions of Sabah by around the fifteenth century through trade, intermarriage, and the influence of the Sultanates of Brunei and Sulu. Over a period of approximately 600 years, Islam spread gradually and was embraced by individuals, families, communities, and, in some instances, entire villages.
Prior to 1963, however, the majority of indigenous peoples living in Sabah's interior—including the Kadazan, Dusun, Murut, Rungus, and other ethnic groups—continued to practise traditional animistic beliefs centred on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and the Creator God, Kinorohingan. From the late nineteenth century onwards, Christian missionaries also succeeded in spreading Christianity throughout many parts of the interior.
As a result, before 1967, Sabah's religious landscape generally reflected Islam as the dominant religion along much of the coastline, while Christianity and traditional indigenous beliefs were more widespread in the interior. Nevertheless, it would be inaccurate to suggest that Islam was entirely absent from the interior before 1963, as Muslim communities and individuals had long been present in certain inland areas.
This situation changed significantly during the administration of Tun Datu Mustapha (1967–1975). Through missionary activities coordinated by the United Sabah Islamic Association (USIA) and supported by the Malaysian Government, the process of Islamisation became more organised and involved large numbers of indigenous people in the interior within a relatively short period. This marked a new phase in the history of Islam in Sabah, differing substantially from the gradual process of religious diffusion that had taken place over the previous six centuries.
When discussing the large-scale conversions to Islam during the administrations of Tun Datu Mustapha and Tun Syed Kechik in the early 1970s, the reference is to the conversion of entire communities, villages, and families within a relatively short period. Several historical studies and contemporary reports also note that some of these conversion efforts were accompanied by offers of financial assistance, federal government development aid, and other forms of incentives made available to participating communities.

Based on my interviews with former USNO and BERJAYA leaders, people who willingly embraced islam in the early 1970s during these mass conversions had a choice and were not necessarily compelled to convert to Islam.
Whether these policies and practices were right or wrong is a matter open to interpretation and is not the focus of my discussion. From my perspective, I examine these events from a historical and sociological standpoint, viewing them as a process of social change that significantly shaped Sabah's religious landscape during the early 1970s.
https://t.co/qCipAxxeIt

Over the past 24 hours, I have had a lot of comments in my inbox about the topic of mass conversions of animist and non-animists between 1970-1975 by the USNO political party in collaboration with the Federal Government .
Many were positive comments and personal stories shared of experiences. Thank you to all who reached out to me. 
In early 2025 I wrote a book titled Zaraland: Sabah Political History between 1961-1976 explaining what transpired during this period before and after Malaysia was formed from a Sabah perspective. 
I know this topic very well including what transpired under Mustapha and Stephens from a political perspective. 
I will however add some notes and observations below to give more context:

1-Mustapha saw himself as some sort of a Sultan of Sabah, especially after Donald Stephens was forced to dissolve the UPKO political party in December 1967. He was unchallenged, had no opposition political party to deal with and was now free to do as he pleased as Chief Minister.
This is recorded in Federalism by Bruce Larsson & in Zaraland: Sabah Political History 1961-1976 with sources and documents to support this historical narrative.

2-In 1969, the emergency was declared throughout Malaysia and each state or territory were tasked to form their own council. This council would be led by the Chief Minister, and key members of the Federal Government and Security Services in that state or territory.
Sabah is the only state to have the emergency in place until mid-1975. No other state had the emergency for as long as it did.

3-Opposition members were arrested under the emergency between 1969-1970 and jailed at Kepayaan Prison. This removed any direct challenge to Mustapha and Syed Kechik who again, ruled unopposed.

4-It was during this period that two things happened.
a-the mass migration of illegal peoples into Sabah from the Southern Philippines began due to conflict with the Marcos Government 
b-the establishment of United Sabah Islamic Association (USIA) and branches throughout Sabah to spread Islam in the interior of Sabah amongst animist and non-animist indigenous peoples. Imams were brought in from West Malaysia and other regions to assist in this event. 

People of various faiths were encouraged to convert to Islam. Some agreed. Others didn't. To my knowledge, they were not forced to do so.
But that was not the case in the local government service: many of Mustapha's people were recruited into government service and those who were in government service and who were not muslim were encouraged to convert. If they did not, many did not progress up the ladder in government or were simply given year to year contracts of employment. They were not permanent government employees with pension.
This was what my family experienced when serving in JKR in the early 1970s. 

Many people today who do not understand or know about what happened during the "dark years" under Mustapha and Syed Kechik are shocked to read the topic and comments over the past 24 hours. It is all new to them and they may have found it hard to believe such things did happen.
But they did.
History has been recorded and it has been told and re-told by Sabahans who experienced these events during the Mustapha-Kechik years. 
One or two individuals have suggested I should not discuss the topic because its "sensitive" and it has been 50 years since it all transpired. However, we should not be afraid to tell Sabah history as truthfully and honestly as possible.
We have our own history. It should not be dictated by the West Malaysians or any Government Agency.
I recently sat at a Federal Government event about the legacy of Tun Fuad Stephens and had to listen to a fair bit of history. Some aspects were incorrect factually. Other aspects were inaccurate Federal Government versions of Sabah political history.
The worrying part is many in the audience were young minds and recent graduates who were told a completely different version of Sabah politics during the Mustapha-Kechik years. 
At the end of the day, we should accept the fact Mustapha made many mistakes as Chief Minister of Sabah. 

So did Donald Stephens. 
Either we keep hiding Sabah history because we are afraid it will upset someone or some people or we tell it honestly as best we can from a historical perspective to remind ourselves of the past so it does not happen again or we can learn from the past the good and bad of Sabah political history. 
 
#Sabah #RealHistory #mustaphaharun

UK Labour Party Looks to Impose Election Restrictions on Social Media to Combat ‘Misinformation’

In other words, only leftist misinformation should be allowed. 😉

They want far-left news sources like the BBC to be the only ones allowed to be trusted sources. 🙄

We went through this during the Biden admin here in the US. 

Many of us were de-platformed for speaking about things that have since been proven to be true.

- Such as covid
- Hunter's laptop 
...and much more

This is what happens when the left is in charge. 😉
Truth is no longer allowed. 

From the article:

The ruling leftist Labour Party in Britain has suggested that social media sites should face new restrictions during elections, as the government claims it wants to confront the spread of so-called misinformation.

Deputy leader of the Labour Party, Lucy Powell, went on a media blitz on Sunday, advocating for the Representation of the People Bill to further clamp down on social media, particularly around elections.

Powell is a close ally of Britain’s likely next Prime Minister, Andy Burnham, the former Mayor of Manchester, who recently parachuted into the House of Commons following outgoing Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer’s disastrous performance at May’s local elections.

Speaking to LBC, she said, “Millions of people get their news from social media, yet our laws haven’t kept pace.”

“The biggest influence on what many voters see during election campaigns is not a TV news bulletin, it’s social media feeds decided by opaque algorithms, where falsehoods, deepfakes and coordinated mis and disinformation can spread at alarming speed, with real-world consequence,” Powell asserted.

The deputy Labour leader said that while freedom of expression is “fundamental” to British democracy, it is important that the government makes sure that “the public can make informed choices based on accurate information.”

Powell’s proposal would extend some of the restrictions currently on British broadcasters during the immediate run-up to elections, also known as “purdah”. This includes a blanket ban on media reporting on election issues on polling day until after the polls have closed. Broadcasters are also required to give equal time to candidates to ensure an even playing field.

It comes in the wake of UK Culture Secretary announcing last week that she would be quitting Elon Musk’s X social media platform and that the government department she runs would do the same.

Nandy said that X was a “platform originally designed for free speech and expression now favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate.”

However, now she claims that the social media firm “isn’t healthy for our democracy or our communities and I don’t want to support it.”

Nandy said that she would continue posting on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn.

Meanwhile, the video hosting platform YouTube warned British users over the weekend that their content likely faces the prospect of being suppressed algorithmically under new rules proposed by the left-wing government.

According to reports, the government is looking to require social media firms to prioritise “trusted” news sources like the BBC over independent media, despite longstanding accusations of bias against the UK public broadcaster and significant scandals over the accuracy of its reporting.

text:
Many friends and fellow creators—both people I know and complete strangers—often ask me whether they should build a Facebook channel to make money through Facebook’s Content Monetization (CM) program.

My answer is usually: No, or at the very least, think very carefully before doing so.

The reason is simple: I never want to invest all my time and effort into building a channel that relies on a revenue source I have no control over.

After nearly 15 years working within the Facebook ecosystem and supporting countless publishers, I’ve learned that Facebook revenue can change dramatically over time. Policies can be updated at any moment, and creators sometimes face situations where their earnings are withheld, monetization features are restricted, or monetization is removed altogether without what they feel is a sufficiently clear explanation.

That makes relying entirely on Content Monetization a strategy with significant risk.

In fact, this pattern has repeated itself with almost every Facebook monetization program over the years—Ad Breaks, Audience Network, Instant Articles, In-Stream Ads, Ads on Reels, Performance Bonus, and now Content Monetization. They usually launch with attractive earning opportunities, gain momentum for a few months, and then Facebook begins tightening enforcement. Many partners eventually experience payment holds or monetization issues despite publishing original, policy-compliant content. The recent wave of demonetization and withheld payments across the creator community made many people realize this firsthand.

Instead of focusing on how much Facebook pays per view, I’ve always believed that building a high-quality audience is far more valuable. That is the real asset behind a successful channel.

People often ask me:

“How do you build a Facebook or Instagram page that gets millions of views?”

Honestly, reaching millions of views isn’t nearly as difficult as it used to be.

What’s much harder is building an audience that genuinely cares about your content—people who willingly come back to watch your future posts.

This is where I see many creators making the same mistake. Today they post about technology, tomorrow about football, then daily life, followed by personal finance or beauty a few days later. A few videos may go viral, but those views rarely create long-term value.

The algorithm struggles to understand who your ideal audience is, and followers don’t know what to expect after clicking the Follow button.

If your goal is simply to create a few viral videos, that’s perfectly fine.

But if your goal is long-term income—especially through Affiliate Marketing or driving traffic to a blog monetized with Google AdX—then the strategy is completely different.

Start by choosing one specific niche and consistently creating content around it. Whether it’s health, beauty, travel, personal finance, cars, technology, pets, or any field you’re knowledgeable or passionate about, stay focused.

When all of your content serves the same audience, Facebook and Instagram’s algorithms become increasingly effective at identifying the right people to show your content to. At the same time, you’ll build a community with shared interests and needs.

When you recommend an affiliate product, your conversion rate will naturally be higher because you’re promoting something that genuinely matches your audience.

When you share an in-depth article on your blog, you’ll attract higher-quality visitors who stay longer and view more pages. Those are exactly the signals that help maximize advertising revenue through Google AdX while creating a more sustainable business over time.

Don’t obsess over view counts.

A video with two million views from the wrong audience may generate less revenue than a channel with only tens of thousands of followers who perfectly match its niche.

In marketing, the right audience will always outperform a larger audience.

text:
Stories do more than teach children to read.

They help children understand themselves.

Their feelings.
Their relationships.
Their place in the world.

That’s why we loved speaking with Early Years educator and children’s author Carla Gunn, creator of "What Letter Shall We Look at Today?: Animals, Emotions, and Stories from A to Z."

Her book brings together three powerful parts of early learning:

🔤 Alphabet development
📖 Storytelling
💭 Emotional literacy

Each letter is paired with an animal, an emotion, and a story, helping children explore language and feelings in a way that feels natural, safe, and engaging.

Because for young children, talking about emotions directly can sometimes feel difficult.
But talking about how a character feels?
That opens the door.

Through stories like Kim the Kind Kangaroo and Maisie the Marvellous Monkey, children are encouraged to explore kindness, responsibility, confidence, curiosity, and self-expression.

These are not just reading skills.

They are life skills.

Carla’s work also supports key areas of the EYFS, including communication and language, personal, social and emotional development, and literacy.

At 1decision, we know that literacy, wellbeing, empathy, resilience, and communication are deeply connected.

When children feel safe, valued, and interested, learning becomes meaningful.

And when stories help children talk about feelings, they become more than books.

They become tools for growth.

Thank you to Carla Gunn for sharing her story and for continuing to support children’s learning, wellbeing, and lifelong love of reading. 📚✨

Streamline News Brief

Monday, 06 July 2026, 08:00 AM WAT

1. ​From consumers to creators: Meet 10 Nigerian AI founders rewriting the tech narrative

Nigeria's AI scene is shifting from consumer to creator: a new generation of founders is building AI systems rooted in African realities, from YarnGPT (text-to-speech in Nigerian languages like Yoruba, Hausa, and Igbo) to Intron Health (medical AI designed for African hospitals). These builders are solving local challenges with global relevance-diagnosing babies' cries, converting doctors' speech into structured medical records, and training language models on indigenous languages. The ecosystem is maturing fast: when a hackathon project like YarnGPT gets acquired by a Nigerian company rather than scooped up by foreign investors, it signals Africa's AI builders are finally writing their own narrative.

Source: Nairametrics | https://t.co/YUEyiAI6lf

2. What to expect from Nigeria's economy in the second half of 2026

Nigeria's economy showed promise in H1 2026 with GDP growth at 3.89% and inflation dropping from 34% to 15%-but geopolitical tensions pushing up oil prices have reversed that trend, with inflation climbing back to 15.93% by May. Political spending ahead of 2027 elections adds another risk factor as the country depends heavily on volatile commodity prices. How policymakers handle these competing pressures in the second half of 2026 will determine whether Africa's fourth-largest economy achieves sustainable growth or slides back into turbulence.

Source: Businessday NG | https://t.co/5OHmY5Yxbk

3. Why silence from Tinubu on Adeniyi and Gbajabiamila carries risk, By Adeola Akinremi

A controversy surrounding Nigeria's Presidential Economic Advisory Council-criticized as a "ghost agency"-has intensified after the Presidency publicly discredited accuser Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew while exonerating President Tinubu's Chief of Staff, Femi Gbajabiamila. Rather than settling the matter, the swift political response has shifted focus from the allegations themselves to a deeper concern: whether Nigeria's institutions can investigate independently without political interference. The article argues that an independent inquiry is now essential-not to determine guilt, but to restore the institutional credibility that markets and democracy depend on.

Source: Premium Times Nigeria | https://t.co/IwLehEKokt

text:
Content creation today = meaning making = art of persuasion.

In this article, Dan broke down the content creation SOP into 3 human tensions and 5 levers. it's brilliantly articulated. below are my thoughts after reading this article (not generated by AI).

3 human tensions are:

a. survival tension - doesn't necessarily mean you are simply fighting for next month's rent. existential anxiety can be categorized as survival tension here as well.

b. identity tension - this is the primal tribal desire we all share.

c. progress tension - Maslow's hierarchy of needs; we want to feel like we're on track toward something greater.

5 levers are:

1. name the threat (survival tension). naming a threat seems crucial for overcoming the fear of it. it's a switch you flip by telling yourself: i'm gonna face this demon of my heart today. it's like how Harry Potter can say Lord Voldemort's name while others just call him 'you-know-who'.

2. mirror the identity (identity tension): identify yourself with a certain group of people. this is why so many people use 'hey founders/builders/creators…' to start a post.

3. exclude people (identity tension, deepened): exclusivity creates a sense of belonging. this is how private/elite clubs work.

4. paint the transformation (progress tension): this is why 'build in public' and 'learn in public' content works. by tracking someone else's progress, we feel part of their journey; it also triggers a sense of urgency - if he/she can do this, then i can too.

5. give the first step (progress tension, activated): if lever 4 shows the destination, this one removes the friction of starting. it's not enough to make someone want the transformation - you have to hand them the one small move they can make right now. the smaller and more concrete the first step, the more likely they act.

text:
Our first beta user for Sauce was B2B creator @kaleighf.

As a creator who specializes in AEO (and, largely, LinkedIn for AEO) she wanted to prove the methods she was preaching were actually working.

Enter Sauce 👋🌶️

Kaleigh's question: "I'm putting a lot of effort into my LinkedIn articles and newsletter - is the content actually driving citations and mentions?"

Over the past month, we've tracked every LinkedIn article Kaleigh has published against a set of core prompts/buyer questions most important to her and her work.

Here are our biggest insights so far:

1. Not only are her articles earning citations for her core prompts, but the citations are *sticking*. Her top 5 articles have been consistently cited across 25%-50% of our total runs.

2. LinkedIn articles have staying power, babe. That spike around June 11? Her older articles started earning even more citations as her newer articles were starting to spike.

3. LinkedIn articles are being cited fast. Her most recent LinkedIn article was being cited for one of her core prompts less than 24 hours after she published it.

4. Even with off-site content, it's worth it to set a smaller list of core prompts first, then create something worth citing that maps back to your core list. Which prompts and buyer questions are actually most important to your team and business? Everything else is just noise.

5. It's SO helpful to attach off-site content to the person creating it. This gives you extra insight into *why* the content may be working and whether you can apply it across your other advocates out there creating and engaging across other off-site platforms like LinkedIn, YouTube, Reddit, and G2.

Kaleigh's on these https://t.co/AMFc4efFPa streets proving out the value of LinkedIn for AEO.

Our other beta users, marketing leaders from some of my favorite brands, are currently tracking over 400+ pieces of off-site content across Reddit, YouTube, LinkedIn, and more.

SEO, community, and brand leaders, your people - your employee and customer advocates, your creators like Kaleigh Moore - they are the secret sauce of this next era of AEO 🤌

Come let us help you prove it.

text:
if feel like you’ve run out of ad creative ideas… chances are.. you haven’t run out of ideas… you’ve run out of a system for generating them

i’ve used this exact framework to help teams go from posting the same 3 angles on repeat to running 1,000+ unique creative combinations off a single persona

before i drop the 4 buckets, understand this…

creative burnout is almost always a mental model or framework problem

most people don’t lack creativity..

you just lack the specific buckets and triggers that make new ideas obvious instead of forced

i’ll show you the math at the end so you can see exactly how much this multiplies your output

here’s the 4 buckets to add to your creative matrix (these are the obvious ones)

1. awareness levels

when i start working with a new team, unless they have an experienced marketer in-house, almost all of their ads are written at the same awareness level…

…which means there are huge pockets of buyers they’ve never spoken to, simply because they never spoke to them differently

there are 5 awareness levels, so just that times the 1 persona you’re speaking to opens up a whole new world of creative

2. not using “moments in time” when targeting different personas

the most amount of your time as a marketer should go into research…

…and within that research you’ll find key moments in time for your icp that act as catalysts for action…

…so speaking to these specific moments will make your prospects feel very understood, which leads to more consumption, which leads to more conversions

3. hook mechanics

so obviously yes, everyone knows to test different hooks…

…but the real unlock is being able to categorize your hooks

so instead of just knowing “this hook worked,” it’s understanding at the fundamental level WHY it worked

do fear based hooks work better than comparison hooks for you? 

do social proof hooks give you lower cpa while relatability hooks bring in more clicks?

utilizing hook mechanics and then being able to decipher the downstream effects is a major unlock you want in your back pocket as a marketer

4. formats

the most obvious, yet severely untapped by tons of marketers

each icp consumes content differently…

…which means there are formats certain audiences will resonate with, and others won’t

ugc talking head, memes, pov, day in the life, scientific breakdown, etc

using this to maximize your output will just show you how small you’ve been thinking and approaching your creative process

now here’s the math i promised

5 awareness levels

times 7 key moments in time for one persona

times 5 hook mechanics

times 6 winning formats

5 x 7 x 5 x 6 = 1,050 unique creative angles… from 
one persona

and that’s just ONE persona

now imagine you target a sub avatar, or a completely different icp

that’s not 1,050 anymore, that’s 1,050 x however many segments you actually sell to

most marketers are sitting on 5-10x the creative volume they think they have, they just never built the model to see it

if there’s only 2 things you take from this, let it be these

1. use this mental model, or build your own… but stop creating from a blank page

2. do more research, because the model is only as good as what you feed it

the buckets multiply your output, but research is what fills them

if there is no research… then no moments in time, no awareness levels to write to, no hook data to categorize

the system doesn’t replace the work, it just makes sure the work compounds

if you read this and realized you’ve been “out of ideas” this whole time… it was never a creativity problem

it was a skill issue

text:
this $10k/mo SaaS startegy works on instagram. here's the exact same play but for X...

1. fix your profile first. 

your bio should say the outcome your product delivers, not your job title, and your pinned tweet should be a product demo or your best result. every person who likes one of your posts will check this page before following, so treat it like your landing page... because that's exactly what it is.

2. build a swipe file.

spend one hour finding 10 accounts in your niche whose posts consistently print, then save their top 20 posts each. that's 200 proven structures you can pull from forever. this is the "copy your competitors" step, and the rule is simple: you steal the skeleton, never the words.

3. post at least 2x every day, and push for 4-5x once you find your rhythm. 

every post is a proven structure from your swipe file filled with YOUR product's real numbers, because "we cut onboarding from 14 days to 3" will always beat generic saas wisdom. building in public is the UGC of X... your metrics are the content, and volume is what gets the algorithm to take you seriously.

4. reply 20x every day. 

under 5k followers, replies ARE your distribution, not your posts. find the 10-15 big accounts your buyers already follow and be the smartest reply under their posts every single day. this is where the followers actually come from, and skipping it is why most founder accounts stay dead.

5. every week, publish one article and one lead magnet. 

the article is a full breakdown of something real (how you got your first 10 users) and it becomes your most bookmarked asset. the lead magnet is a free resource your ICP actually wants (template, checklist, teardown) that turns your reach into DMs and emails instead of just likes.

6. DM everyone who engages three times or more. 

no pitch... just "hey, saw you liked the onboarding post, are you dealing with the same thing?" that's the whole message. conversations become demos, and demos become revenue.

that's the entire system: 

> 2-5 posts +
> 20 replies +
> 1 article + 
> 1 lead magnet per week + 
> DMs

if the instagram version isn't for you, this one runs from your desk with zero video editing.

steal it.

text:
Mexico’s World Cup squad have returned a collection of Rolex watches gifted to them by American content creator Stevewilldoit.

Stephen Deleonardis, known as Stevewilldoit online, where he has over 1.5million subscribers on YouTube, presented every member of the Mexico squad with a watch at their training base in the build up to their last-32 tie against Ecuador.

Mexico said in a statement posted on the team’s official social media channels that by “mutual agreement” the squad decided to return the watches to Deleonardis, which were gifted by the content creator “on his own initiative”.

Asked by midfielder Obed Vargas why he had presented the team with the gift, Deleonardis explained he lived in Mexico and bet $2m on the side to beat Ecuador in the last-32, which he said earlier in the video would return a $1m profit - the supposed total value of the watches.

Restrictions around offering and accepting gifts are laid out in Article 21 of FIFA’s Code of Ethics. World soccer’s governing body states that gifts are prohibited unless they have “symbolic or trivial value”.

More from @LeonImber 

🔗 https://t.co/aZxKNJKeu3

text:
This indie product has only 1 follower on IndieHackers, yet reportedly makes $1,000/month.

It's called Intebwio. Not a big company. Not an influencer. Just a solo dev building an AI search engine.

Here's how it works:

• Instead of showing you 10 links to click through, Intebwio synthesizes all relevant info into one clean page.
• Ask "how to raise a cat" and you get a complete article covering feeding, vaccines, litter boxes — no tab-hopping.
• It uses AI to reorganize search results into a readable summary. (Founder didn't specify the exact model.)

Monetization is dead simple:
• Free tier: limited daily searches.
• Paid tier: unlimited. Around a few dollars/month.

Founder claims steady ~$1k/month MRR. Can't verify — only 1 follower — but the model is clearly working.

The hard parts:
• AI search is expensive. Server & API costs scale fast.
• Competition: Google, Bing, and tons of AI search startups.
• User acquisition: nearly zero marketing. Founder is probably a tech-focused builder.

Takeaway for you:
If you have some tech skills, try a niche AI search tool — legal docs, medical knowledge, a specific hobby. Don't compete with giants; own a tiny vertical.

No coding? It's tougher, but low-code tools and AI APIs make it more accessible.

My take: Intebwio proves "AI + search" can monetize. $1k/month isn't huge in indie dev circles, but it's real. Growth is slow, and costs are tricky.

Good for a side hustle? Maybe. Expect overnight riches? No — it only has one follower.

Follow for more real AI money breakdowns.

text:
Spent most of the last year building OTHER brands and somewhat let my own personal one slip... 🤔

All those tall blue spikes with nothing underneath? That's me dropping an article, going viral for a day, then disappearing for two weeks to go work on other projects my time was heavily allocated to.

Big numbers, thin consistency, marginal growth as a result. 

Now look at the last couple of weeks (red arrow)

The density is back! Daily posting, daily replies, daily engagement.. Not just article spikes into silence.

The difference isn't strategy, it's that I've got the time AND the passion back after my break. Turns out you can't fake consistency, and you can't outsource your personal brand to anyone but yourself.

Still, it was hands off and big hits from the research I released, and I still pulled in nearly 6m impressions - I should be able to at least double that now.

+ Google just releases search profiles, and you can only claim them yours once you hit 100K, so I've got a real reason to go for it now! 🙌

I still never monetized this account either, same with my YT or any of my SEO "influencer" socials, looking at some of the earnings on here recently though, maybe that was a mistake 😅

text:
20 Powerful NotebookLM Prompts.

To Learn Faster, Think Smarter & Research Like a Pro.👇

1. Smart Summary
 Turn lengthy documents into clear insights.
 👉 “Summarize this material into the 10 most important ideas, arguments, and actionable takeaways in simple language.”

2. Explain Like I’m New
Make difficult topics easy to grasp.
👉 “Explain this topic for a complete beginner using simple examples, analogies, and step-by-step explanations.”

3. Advanced Breakdown
Go beyond surface-level understanding.
   👉 “Break this content into core ideas, hidden assumptions, expert insights, and details most people overlook.”

4. Source Comparison
   Find patterns and contradictions.
 👉 “Compare these sources and highlight where they agree, disagree, and what unique value each one adds.”

5. Instant Study Notes
   Create organized notes in seconds.
 👉 “Convert this content into structured study notes with headings, bullets, definitions, and examples.”

6. Flashcard Creator
   Learn through active recall.
 👉 “Generate 25 useful flashcards from this material with clear questions and concise answers.”

7. Interactive Quiz
   Test your understanding.
  👉 “Create a quiz that progresses from beginner to advanced based only on this source. Grade my answers afterward.”

8. Memory Techniques
   Retain information faster.
 👉 “Create mnemonics, memory hooks, and analogies to help me remember the key concepts.”

9. Timeline Generator
   See the bigger picture clearly.
 👉 “Extract all major events, milestones, and developments from these sources and organize them into a timeline.”

10. Evidence & Quotes
    Pull out the strongest insights.
  👉 “Find the most impactful quotes, statistics, and supporting evidence from these materials.”

11. Identify Research Gaps
    Discover what’s missing.
  👉 “Point out weak arguments, missing evidence, unanswered questions, and research gaps in these sources.”

12. Debate Viewpoints
    Strengthen critical thinking.
  👉 “Present the strongest arguments both for and against the main idea as if two experts are debating.”

13. Build a Framework
    Turn knowledge into systems.
  👉 “Transform the ideas from these sources into a practical framework, checklist, or repeatable process.”

14. Repurpose Into Content
    Turn research into content assets.
 👉 “Use this material to create a LinkedIn post, article outline, tweet thread, and newsletter concept.”

15. Expert Q&A Mode
    Learn directly from the material.
  👉 “Act like an expert on these uploaded sources. Answer my questions only using the provided information.”

16. Executive Summary
    Get strategic insights quickly.
  👉 “Create a concise 5-minute executive briefing with key insights, implications, and action points.”

17. Learning Roadmap
  Turn information into a curriculum.
  👉 “Convert this notebook into a 7-day learning plan with lessons, exercises, and checkpoints.”

18. Idea Expansion
    Unlock creative applications.
  👉 “Generate 20 original ideas, opportunities, or innovations inspired by these materials.”

19. Teach It Simply
 Prepare to explain it to others.
👉 “Rewrite the key concepts into a simple teaching script I can explain in under 5 minutes.”

20. Execution Plan 
Move from learning to action.
👉 “Create a practical action plan with priorities, next steps, and deadlines based on these sources.”

❤️ Save for later
🔁 Share with your network
🔖 Bookmark this thread

Follow @SaurabhDub28465  for more AI, Tech productivity & research workflows.

text:
You'll miss how META PIXEL is important for a successful Meta Ads campaign if you don't read this post 👇

1. Conversion Tracking

If you don't set up Meta Pixel Events, you won't be able to understand how many conversions came from your ads, such as Purchases, Leads, or any other important Events

2. Ads Optimization

Through Meta Pixel, Meta's algorithm can learn which types of users are most likely to convert. It then optimizes your campaigns by showing your ads more frequently to those users

3. Custom Audiences

Using Meta Pixel Events, you can create different types of Custom Audiences. (For example: Website visitors from the last 30 days)

4. Lookalike Audiences

Using a Custom Audience, you can create a Lookalike Audience. This allows Meta to find many more potential customers who are similar to your website visitors

5. Retargeting

Using Custom/Lookalike Audiences, you can run Retargeting Ads to target users who previously visited your website or saw your ads but didn't convert

6. Content Personalization

Meta now uses the same off-site activity data to personalize what users see in their Facebook and Instagram Feeds, as well as what Meta AI recommends to them

7. Signals for Machine Learning

Meta Pixel data helps Meta's AI model understand user behavior, allowing it to improve conversion prediction, targeting, and campaign optimization over time

8. Meta Conversions API

When Meta Pixel is used together with the Conversions API, data lost due to browser restrictions, cookie limitations, and ad blockers can be recovered. As a result, more accurate conversion tracking becomes possible

9. Campaign Reporting

Meta Pixel helps you understand which Campaign, Ad Set, or Ad generated a conversion

10. Campaign Measurement

Meta Pixel helps measure ROAS, CPA, Conversion Rate, and other important performance metrics, making it easier to evaluate your campaign's performance

#MetaAds #MetaPixel #ConversionTracking

We, the people of the United States of America, solemnly announce today: we are going to be independent! Whoever loves to be a British grandson will continue to be a grandson. We will not serve you!

The first truth of the universe: all people are born equal!

Article 2: The Creator (God) has given us several inalienable rights - the right to life, the right to freedom, and the right to pursue happiness! ( Note that it is ”pursuit“, not ”guaranteing happiness lying flat“)

The government was created by the people to protect these rights. Its power comes from the consent of the ruled!

If any government dares to eat dry food, fool around, and violate everyone‘s rights, then the people have the natural right to overthrow it. Change to a reliable one!

We can’t stand the tyranny of the King of England! The following is his old man‘s ”great achievements“ (manual dog head):

• Reject us to pass beneficial laws

• Don’t let others hold a parliament, and disband the parliament.

• Send a bunch of soldiers to eat rice and grab territory.

• Cut off our trade with the whole world

• Collect all kinds of strange taxes from us (learn about tea tax)

We have bitterly persuaded him countless times, but as a result, the old man not only didn’t listen, but also became worse!

Brothers, it‘s time!

From now on, we will break with the British royal family!
